gate_question: Does this exact current protocol, with current live state, allow an unprivileged attacker to extract funds or rewards beyond entitlement?

candidate.title_or_claim: Reentrancy in claim() via PORTAL_ROLLER delegatecall — potential double-claim of LP fees
candidate.claimed_root_cause: No reentrancy guard on claim(); plain receive(); roller's claim logic (CEI ordering) not available in repository
candidate.claimed_impact: Attacker as token beneficiary re-enters claim() before roller clears state, draining LP fees multiple times

live_preconditions.name: attacker_is_token_beneficiary
live_preconditions.observed_value: unknown — _tokenBeneficiaries mapping not enumerable from live_context.json
live_preconditions.command_if_missing: cast call 0xe2ce6ab80874fa9fa2aae65d277dd6b8e65c9de0 'getLocks(address)(uint256[])' <token> --rpc-url $BSC_RPC; then check _tokenBeneficiaries[token] via storage slot keccak256(abi.encode(token, 169))

live_preconditions.name: token_has_accumulated_lp_fees
live_preconditions.observed_value: unknown — lpLocks mapping not enumerable from live_context.json

live_preconditions.name: roller_does_not_use_cei_pattern
live_preconditions.observed_value: unknown — PORTAL_ROLLER source not present in repository; only IRoller interface available

source_code_basis.why_it_matters: claim() has no reentrancy guard and unconditionally delegatecalls PORTAL_ROLLER; if roller sends ETH to beneficiary before clearing state, re-entry is possible
Plain payable receive with no guard; relevant only for ETH flowing into Portal from locker, not the reentrancy callback itself
Raw assembly delegatecall with no reentrancy lock; roller logic runs in Portal's storage context
_tokenBeneficiaries (slot 169) and lpLocks (slot 165) are the state variables the roller must clear before any ETH transfer to prevent reentrancy

attacker_path.actor: Unprivileged attacker who is the current _tokenBeneficiaries[token] for a token with accumulated LP fees
call_sequence: 1. Attacker deploys MaliciousReceiver whose receive() calls Portal.claim(token) and tracks depth
2. Attacker (or token creator) sets MaliciousReceiver as _tokenBeneficiaries[token] via setTokenBeneficiary()
4. Portal delegatecalls PORTAL_ROLLER in Portal's storage context
6. Roller calls locker collectFees() — locker sends ETH to Portal (triggers Portal.receive(), not attacker's receive())
7. Roller sends collected ETH to beneficiary (MaliciousReceiver) — THIS triggers attacker's receive()
8. MaliciousReceiver.receive() re-enters Portal.claim(token) before roller clears state
9. If roller has not yet updated _tokenBeneficiaries or a claimed flag, beneficiary check passes again and fees are re-collected
state_after: If vulnerable: BeneficiaryClaimed emitted multiple times; Portal ETH balance drained beyond single legitimate fee amount
If protected: second claim reverts or returns (0,0) due to CEI in roller

extraction_analysis.asset_or_reward: LP fees (ETH) claimable by token beneficiary via claim()
extraction_analysis.attacker_gain: Multiple payouts of the same accumulated LP fee amount in a single transaction
extraction_analysis.victim_or_protocol_loss: Portal proxy ETH balance (1361+ BNB live) or locker-held LP fee ETH drained beyond single entitlement
extraction_analysis.why_gain_exceeds_entitlement: Beneficiary is entitled to claim accumulated fees once per collection cycle; reentrancy before state clearance allows repeated collection of the same fees

setup: Fork BSC at block 108382650 against proxy 0xe2ce6ab80874fa9fa2aae65d277dd6b8e65c9de0
Resolve PORTAL_ROLLER address: cast call proxy 'PORTAL_ROLLER()(address)' and fetch its verified source
Identify token where lpLocks[token] is non-zero and has accumulated fees via getLocks(token)
Deploy MaliciousReceiver: receive() calls Portal.claim(token) once on re-entry and records depth and ETH received
Use vm.store to set _tokenBeneficiaries[token] = address(MaliciousReceiver) at slot keccak256(abi.encode(token, 169))
Record Portal.balance and MaliciousReceiver.balance before attack
transaction_sequence: On ETH receipt in MaliciousReceiver.receive(), re-enter Portal.claim(token) once
Record total ETH received by MaliciousReceiver and BeneficiaryClaimed event count
expected_assertions: If vulnerable: BeneficiaryClaimed emitted more than once; MaliciousReceiver received more ETH than single legitimate fee amount
If protected: second claim() call reverts with reentrancy guard error, or returns (0,0) because roller cleared state before ETH transfer (CEI)
local_proof_required.reject_if_assertion_fails: If second claim() reverts or returns (0,0) on re-entry, roller uses CEI pattern and the candidate is REJECT

rejection_reason: Cannot promote beyond NEEDS_LOCAL_PROOF: (1) PORTAL_ROLLER source is absent from the repository — the single blocking unknown is whether the roller clears _tokenBeneficiaries or a claimed-flag before sending ETH to the beneficiary; (2) live preconditions (attacker-controlled beneficiary with accumulated fees) are not confirmed from available live context; (3) the candidate report's attack path description contains a logical flaw — Portal's receive() being triggered by the locker sending ETH to Portal does NOT trigger the attacker's receive(); the actual reentrancy callback fires when the roller sends ETH to the beneficiary, which is a separate step the report conflates with Portal's receive(); (4) exact_code_path_exists and concrete_fund_or_reward_gain cannot be set true without roller source or a passing fork test.
